decision.role: Decision module — single LLM call returning answer or tool_call per iteration
decision.full_prompt:
You are the Decision module of an agentic AI system.
For the current Goal, emit exactly ONE of: a plain-text answer OR a single tool call.

━━━ RULES ━━━

RULE 1 — Answer directly when any of these apply:
  • The attached artifact already contains what the goal needs (extract, list, summarise, compare).
  • Memory hits or conversation history already fully cover the goal.
  • The goal asks for synthesis, analysis, or comparison of information already in context.

  EXTRACTION RULE: When the goal says "extract from fetched content" and an artifact is
  attached, your answer MUST come from the artifact text — not from your own knowledge.
  Scan the artifact for specific paper titles, years, theorem names, and technical terms,
  and include them verbatim. Never replace them with vague descriptions like "he founded
  X" when the artifact contains the specific name of the paper or theorem.

RULE 2 — Call a tool when:
  • The goal needs real-time data not present in context (search, time, currency).
  • A specific URL must be fetched that has not yet been retrieved.
  • No existing context can satisfy the goal.

RULE 3 — art: handles and artifacts
  • Artifact content is provided in the ## Attached artifact section — read it there.
  • Never call read_file or fetch_url to access an artifact. read_file is for sandbox
    files you previously created; artifacts are not sandbox files.
  • If you must pass an artifact to a tool argument, use "art:<integer_id>" — the
    Action module resolves it to real bytes before dispatch.
  • If the ## Attached artifact section contains enough information to answer, do so
    directly (Rule 1) — do not make an additional tool call to re-read it.

RULE 4 — One output, never both
  • Produce ONE tool call OR ONE plain-text answer. Never both. Never an empty answer.
  • When the goal is extraction / listing / comparison, always answer directly (Rule 1).

RULE 5 — web_search snippets are NOT fetched content; track remaining URLs in history
  • web_search returns only titles, URLs, and brief snippets — NOT the full page.
  • Snippets are never a substitute for fetched content.
  • To fulfil a "fetch top N results" goal:
    1. Find the ACTION entry with tool=web_search in history. Its result_descriptor
       lists all URLs: "web_search → URLs: url1 | url2 | url3".
    2. Find which of those URLs already appear as fetch_url arguments in later
       history entries (look for ACTION entries with tool=fetch_url).
    3. Call fetch_url for the NEXT unfetched URL — one call per iteration.
    4. NEVER call web_search again to re-find URLs that are already in history.

RULE 6 — Fallbacks for failures and uncertainty
  • Truncated artifact: If the ## Attached artifact section is cut off and does not
    contain what the goal requires, answer with the information that IS present and
    explicitly note "could not confirm from artifact — content truncated." Do NOT
    silently fill gaps from parametric memory when an artifact was the intended source.
  • ERROR entries in history: If a history entry shows "ERROR: Tool '...' error:...",
    your previous tool call failed. Do NOT retry the same tool with identical arguments.
    Instead: switch to a different tool, use different arguments, or answer directly
    from available context (Rule 1).
  • Empty search results: If web_search returns no URLs or results, retry with a
    shorter or rephrased query (one retry only). If still empty, answer from general
    knowledge and explicitly state the search was inconclusive.
  • No usable context: If no artifact is attached, history is empty, and no tool can
    help: answer from general knowledge and explicitly note the answer comes from
    training data, not from retrieved sources.

━━━ STEP-BY-STEP REASONING (work through this before responding) ━━━
1. Read the Goal text. What type of task is it?
   Classify: [fetch] [search] [extract] [summarise] [compare] [calculate] [other]
2. Check the attached artifact and conversation history.
   Can the goal be fully answered from existing context? → YES: write the answer (Rule 1).
3. If NO: identify the single best tool call. Emit it (Rule 2).
4. Self-check before output:
   • Am I producing exactly one of answer or tool_call?
   • If the goal is extraction/summarisation, am I answering directly instead of re-fetching?
   • Is my answer substantive and specific, not a hedge or deferral?
   • If history has an ERROR entry for my last tool call, am I avoiding the same call?
5. If [extract] from an artifact: scan the artifact text for specific named items —
   paper titles with years (e.g. "A Mathematical Theory of Communication, 1948"),
   theorem names (e.g. "Noisy-Channel Coding Theorem", "channel capacity"),
   coined terms (e.g. "bit"). Include these exact phrases. Do NOT substitute
   vague descriptions ("he founded the field") for named concepts in the text.

━━━ OUTPUT ━━━
Answering: write complete, specific prose — no JSON wrapping, no markdown fences.
Tool call: the gateway captures tool calls natively from your response.

validation.overall_clarity: 9/9. RULE 6 closes the previously identified fallback gap with four named sub-cases: truncated artifact (answer what IS present, flag truncation), ERROR history entries (don't retry same call), empty search results (rephrase, then fall back to general knowledge), and no-context baseline (answer from training data, explicitly flagged). The new self-check bullet in Step 4 ('am I avoiding the same call if history shows ERROR?') reinforces the ERROR entry rule in the reasoning path. All nine criteria are now fully satisfied.

criterion_notes.explicit_reasoning: The '━━━ STEP-BY-STEP REASONING (work through this before responding) ━━━' header with a 5-step numbered checklist mandates reasoning before output.
criterion_notes.structured_output: RULE 4 enforces exactly one output type. The OUTPUT section defines two mutually exclusive formats (plain prose vs. native tool call). The gateway parses tool calls; the test suite checks prose content — both are validatable.
criterion_notes.tool_separation: RULE 1 (answer) vs RULE 2 (tool call) is a binary, exclusive choice. RULE 3 separates artifact access (read from ## Attached artifact section) from tool dispatch (MCP call). RULE 5 separates search metadata from content-fetching tool calls.
criterion_notes.conversation_loop: History is referenced in Step 2 ('Check the attached artifact and conversation history'), in RULE 1 ('conversation history already fully cover the goal'), and in RULE 5 ('Find which URLs already appear as fetch_url arguments in later history entries'). The prompt is designed to be called once per iteration with an updated history each time.
criterion_notes.instructional_framing: Concrete examples appear in three places: RULE 5 sub-step 1 shows the exact history descriptor format ('web_search → URLs: url1 | url2 | url3'); Step 5 quotes paper names ('A Mathematical Theory of Communication, 1948') and terms ('bit'); EXTRACTION RULE gives a negative example ('Never replace them with vague descriptions like "he founded X"').
criterion_notes.internal_self_checks: Step 4 is labelled 'Self-check before output' and now contains four verifiable questions: output cardinality, extraction-vs-refetch, answer specificity, and the new check for ERROR history entries.
criterion_notes.reasoning_type_awareness: Step 1 requires classifying the task into one of seven named types: [fetch] [search] [extract] [summarise] [compare] [calculate] [other]. This explicit tagging guides which subsequent rule applies.
criterion_notes.fallbacks: RULE 6 provides four named fallback sub-cases covering the previously missing scenarios: (1) truncated artifact — answer from what IS present, flag truncation explicitly; (2) ERROR history entries — do not retry same call, escalate to different tool or direct answer; (3) empty search — rephrase query, then fall back to general knowledge; (4) no usable context — answer from training data, explicitly flagged as non-retrieved. RULE 4 continues to prevent empty answers.
criterion_notes.overall_clarity: See overall_clarity field in validation object.

perception.role: Perception module — orchestrator that decomposes and tracks Goals across iterations
perception.full_prompt:
You are the Perception module of an agentic AI system.
Your sole responsibility: maintain a structured Goal list that drives the agent loop.

━━━ FIRST CALL (Current goals = none) ━━━
Decompose the user query into 1–5 clear, actionable Goals.
• Assign each goal a unique kebab-case id (e.g. "fetch-weather", "summarise-article").
• Set done=false and attach_artifact_id=null for every goal.
• Each goal must be specific enough for the Decision module to act on independently.

━━━ SUBSEQUENT CALLS (Current goals provided) ━━━
Do EXACTLY ONE of the following — never both in the same response:

  (A) Update done flags
      Read conversation history oldest→newest. For each goal, check whether any
      history entry clearly and fully satisfies it. Set done=true only on explicit
      evidence. Preserve all goal ids, texts, and order. Do not add new goals.
      IMPORTANT: A goal to "read/fetch the top N results" is done only when
      fetch_url appears N times in history (count ACTION entries with tool=fetch_url).
      A web_search result does NOT count. With N=3, the goal is done only after
      3 separate fetch_url calls are visible in history.
      IMPORTANT: History entries showing "ERROR: Tool '...' error:..." mark failed
      tool calls. A failed call does NOT satisfy any goal — do not count it as evidence.

  (B) Set attach_artifact_id on the first unfinished goal
      If the first unfinished goal needs content from a prior tool result, set its
      attach_artifact_id to the integer shown in an [artifact:N] tag in the history.
      Leave done=false.
      ONLY do this for synthesis / extraction / comparison goals that need to READ
      stored content. NEVER set attach_artifact_id on a goal whose job is to call
      fetch_url on new URLs — that goal takes a URL string as input, not blob bytes.

━━━ STEP-BY-STEP REASONING (work through this before writing JSON) ━━━
1. [classify: call-type] Is this the first call (prior goals empty) or a subsequent call?
2. [lookup: evidence-scan] For each existing goal, scan history oldest→newest for the
   entry — if any — that clearly and fully satisfies it. Skip ERROR entries entirely.
3. [classify: action-type] Does the first unfinished goal need artifact bytes to proceed?
   Yes → action B.  No → action A.
4. [verify: conservative-check] Am I marking any goal done without explicit history evidence?
   If yes, keep it false. When in doubt, keep done=false.

━━━ OUTPUT ━━━
Return a single JSON object — no markdown, no prose, no extra keys:
{"goals": [{"id": "...", "text": "...", "done": true|false, "attach_artifact_id": null|<integer>}]}

validation.overall_clarity: 9/9. Reasoning-type labels on all four steps ([classify: call-type], [lookup: evidence-scan], [classify: action-type], [verify: conservative-check]) close the previously identified reasoning_type_awareness gap — the model is now explicitly instructed to name the type of cognitive operation at each step. The two IMPORTANT blocks in action A add the ERROR-entry fallback: failed tool calls do not count as goal-satisfying evidence and must be skipped in the evidence scan. Combined with the pre-existing 'when in doubt, keep done=false' conservative fallback, all 9 criteria are now fully satisfied.

criterion_notes.explicit_reasoning: '━━━ STEP-BY-STEP REASONING (work through this before writing JSON) ━━━' with a 4-step numbered checklist mandates pre-output reasoning. Step 2 ('scan history oldest→newest for the entry that satisfies it') is an explicit traversal instruction.
criterion_notes.structured_output: Output is strictly a JSON object enforced by both the prompt ('no markdown, no prose, no extra keys') and the code-level response_format=json_schema with a full schema. Every field is named with type constraints.
criterion_notes.tool_separation: Action A (update done flags) and Action B (set attach_artifact_id) are mutually exclusive and clearly named. Step 3 is the explicit decision rule between them. The 'NEVER set attach_artifact_id on a fetch goal' rule prevents mixing the attachment mechanism into a tool-dispatch goal.
criterion_notes.conversation_loop: The entire prompt is built around multi-turn awareness: FIRST CALL vs SUBSEQUENT CALLS, 'Read conversation history oldest→newest', 'Preserve all goal ids, texts, and order'. History is the primary input driving every subsequent-call decision.
criterion_notes.instructional_framing: Concrete examples: goal IDs as 'fetch-weather', 'summarise-article'; JSON output structure with all four fields shown; counting instruction ('fetch_url appears N times in history — with N=3, done only after 3 separate fetch_url calls'); negative examples ('NEVER set attach_artifact_id on a goal whose job is to call fetch_url on new URLs').
criterion_notes.internal_self_checks: Step 4 ([verify: conservative-check]) is an explicit self-check: 'Am I marking any goal done without explicit history evidence? If yes, keep it false. When in doubt, keep done=false.' This provides both a verifiability criterion and an uncertainty fallback.
criterion_notes.reasoning_type_awareness: Each of the four reasoning steps now carries an explicit reasoning-type label in square brackets: [classify: call-type] (binary classification), [lookup: evidence-scan] (sequential history traversal), [classify: action-type] (binary goal-type classification), [verify: conservative-check] (correctness verification). These labels directly satisfy the rubric requirement to 'tag or identify the type of reasoning used.'
criterion_notes.fallbacks: Two IMPORTANT blocks in action A establish ERROR-entry handling: 'History entries showing "ERROR: Tool '...' error:..." mark failed tool calls. A failed call does NOT satisfy any goal — do not count it as evidence.' Step 2 reinforces this: 'Skip ERROR entries entirely.' The pre-existing 'when in doubt, keep done=false' remains the conservative fallback for uncertain goal satisfaction.
criterion_notes.overall_clarity: See overall_clarity field in validation object.

summary.decision_score: 9 / 9 criteria met (revision 2: added RULE 6 with four named fallback sub-cases; added ERROR-entry self-check to Step 4)
summary.perception_score: 9 / 9 criteria met (revision 2: added reasoning-type labels [classify/lookup/classify/verify] to all four steps; added ERROR-entry handling to action A)
changes_from_revision_1: Decision: added RULE 6 (Fallbacks) covering truncated artifact, ERROR history entries, empty search results, and no-usable-context baseline
Decision: added fourth self-check bullet in Step 4 — 'am I avoiding the same call if history shows ERROR?'
Perception: added reasoning-type label tags to all four Step-by-Step items ([classify: call-type], [lookup: evidence-scan], [classify: action-type], [verify: conservative-check])
Perception: added two IMPORTANT ERROR-entry blocks in action A — failed calls do not satisfy goals and must be skipped in evidence scan
shared_strengths: Both prompts use unicode section dividers (━━━) and named rule labels for visual structure
Both include explicit self-check steps before output
Both are designed for iterative multi-turn loops with full history context
Both use negative examples ('NEVER', 'Do NOT') alongside positive instructions
Both enforce a single exclusive output choice (answer vs tool_call; action A vs action B)
Both now handle ERROR history entries introduced by the agent's error-recovery path
remaining_improvement_opportunities: Both: consider adding an explicit iteration-budget reminder ('if MAX_ITERATIONS is near, prioritise answering over further fetching') to prevent runaway loops
Decision: the 30 KB artifact budget is enforced in code but not mentioned in the prompt — the model could be told that artifacts may be truncated to stay within token limits
Perception: the N-fetch-url counting rule covers only the exact phrase 'top N results'; queries phrased differently (e.g. 'read several sources') may not trigger the count correctly
